title: EOE 169 or 43
wikitext:
[]: EOE 16:9 or 4:3?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sat Feb 15, 2014 9:57 am I've seen two versions of End of Evangelion, one in 4:3 and the other in 16:9. I'm wondering which is the best way to view it. I'm guessing it's similar to the DBZ movies where they were animated in 4:3 but with 16:9 in mind therefore having all the important parts of the image in the centre making cropping not an issue. Still want to make sure though.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Feb 15, 2014 10:24 am DBZ was not animated with 16:9 in mind. That is 100% Funimation fucking shit up like the idiots that they are. It is downright criminal what they have done to the show, especially with the latest release where they not only crop it but also DNR all life out of it. EoE is a theatrical feature and was made with the Vista 1.85 aspect ratio, which was common for film. This is slighly wider than 16:9 (1.77) and that's what the DVDs have. It's been forever since I've seen it so I don't remember if it's cropped to 16:9 or letterboxed to original 1.85. The Manga Ent DVD is shit which has 16:9 letterboxed inside 4:3.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Feb 15, 2014 10:37 am 1.85:1 aspect ratio if you can find it. It might be formatted under a "16:9" setting, which would still have very narrow, almost unnoticeable black bars at the top and bottom of a 16:9 TV preserving the original aspect ratio. I'm not sure how the film was formatted for the Japanese DVD releases. I'm sure it was 16:9 with narrow black bars preserving the 1.85:1 aspect ratio. (I know a lot of Miyazaki's 1.85:1 films did the same thing in their DVD releases.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Feb 15, 2014 11:02 am The film is actually 16x9, but some knucklehead at Manga Entertainment mastered both Death & Rebirth and The End of Evangelion as matted 16x9, so it's a letterbox image sitting on a 4:3 video. Doi.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Feb 15, 2014 11:05 am Yeah, the quality of the Manga Ent. DVD is barely better than if someone had transferred a VHS tape to DVD. Avoid that one of you can. (Unfortunately, it's the only American release of the film. People have gotten used to getting rather creative in finding ways to buy a Japanese copy and getting English subs for it.)

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Feb 15, 2014 5:39 pm Fuck Mangle Entertardment. Bunch of fucks that just don't care. That DVD release is by far the shittiest I've ever seen. It's almost as if they just figuratively mollywopped Anno in the face. But, yeah if a film was made in the 16:9 ratio, then that's the only acceptable format that it should be purchased and viewed in.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Feb 15, 2014 6:05 pm Kind of sounds like a decision someone with a Obsessive Compulsive Disorder would come up with The whole show was in 4:3 therefore the movie must be too ! I really don't see any other reason why anyone would do something like that, furthermore I'm kind of surprised, that this is actually the only version published in America.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Feb 15, 2014 6:49 pm Gorbatschow wrote: Kind of sounds like a decision someone with a Obsessive Compulsive Disorder would come up with The whole show was in 4:3 therefore the movie must be too !

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Feb 15, 2014 6:58 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: You want a horror show, check out their DVD for Patlabor Movie 1: http://i.minus.com/ibkyIlKF9hpBbj.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by LegionWrex » Sat Feb 15, 2014 7:04 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: You want a horror show, check out their DVD for Patlabor Movie 1: http://i.minus.com/ibkyIlKF9hpBbj.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sat Feb 15, 2014 8:14 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: DBZ was not animated with 16:9 in mind. That is 100% Funimation fucking shit up like the idiots that they are. It is downright criminal what they have done to the show, especially with the latest release where they not only crop it but also DNR all life out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Wanderlust » Sat Feb 15, 2014 8:19 pm I actually liked the main menu on the Manga Entertainment release, even though it had like the shortest loop ever. Then again, I'd probably like any menu where "Jesus bleibet meine Freude" plays. More importantly, this topic has reminded me that End of Evangelion STILL hasn't come out on Blu-ray. *sigh* It'd be so nice to have an updated, definitive version of that glorious film.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Feb 15, 2014 8:46 pm I missed that you specifically said movies. I blame Funimation for being such rage-inducing shitstains. Either way EoE is still Vista from the storyboard phase onward.

[]:             Quote Post by LegionWrex » Sat Feb 15, 2014 8:47 pm Wanderlust wrote: More importantly, this topic has reminded me that End of Evangelion STILL hasn't come out on Blu-ray. *sigh* It'd be so nice to have an updated, definitive version of that glorious film.

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sat Feb 15, 2014 8:58 pm I think the main problem is that it costs like 2 million or something.

[]:             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Sun Feb 16, 2014 12:44 am My friend gave me a version that was upres'ed to 720p and in the 2.35x1 ratio. It's basically a stretched out 16x9 copy. To be honest, I kinda prefer it in 2.35 form (I just have a knack for that format, for some reason), since the distortion is barely noticeable, but I can just switch the aspect ratio back to 16x9 on VLC if i wanted to. But to answer your question, see it in 16x9. It's generally the best way to see it, since it was the intended format, but try it in different ratios if you can, and see what you like most.

[]:             Quote Post by HeeroYuyIII » Sun Feb 16, 2014 4:37 pm Who cares? The movie still looks piss poor animation/art wise. My friend had a debate with the creator of this site on Twitter and he is absolutely right. EOE looks like a low budget OVA compared to something like Gundam 0083, Gundam 8th MS Team or Gundam Wing Endless Waltz which all look near movie quality and have animation like syrup.

[]:             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Sun Feb 16, 2014 5:42 pm HeeroYuyIII wrote: EOE looks like a low budget OVA

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sun Feb 16, 2014 5:59 pm ^^ I seriously don't know if you're the best the worst troll I ever saw. You pulled a good one with the Ova, though.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Feb 16, 2014 6:00 pm Justacrazyguy wrote: Hilarious, just freakin hilarious.
